11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 05.05.2017
13: // Description: CSR Register File as specified by RISC-V
14: 
15: import ariane_pkg::*;
16: 
17: module csr_regfile #(
18:     parameter int          ASID_WIDTH      = 1,
19:     parameter int unsigned NR_COMMIT_PORTS = 2
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
22:     input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
24:     // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
25:     output logic                  flush_o,
26:     output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested
27:     // commit acknowledge
28:     input  scoreboard_entry_t [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0] commit_instr_i, // the instruction we want to commit
29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception
34:     input exception_t             ex_i,                       // We've got an exception from the commit stage, take its
35: 
36:     input  fu_op                  csr_op_i,                   // Operation to perform on the CSR file
37:     input  logic  [11:0]          csr_addr_i,                 // Address of the register to read/write
38:     input  logic  [63:0]          csr_wdata_i,                // Write data in
39:     output logic  [63:0]          csr_rdata_o,                // Read data out
40:     input  logic                  dirty_fp_state_i,           // Mark the FP sate as dirty
41:     input  logic                  csr_write_fflags_i,         // Write fflags register e.g.: we are retiring a floating point instruction
42:     input  logic  [63:0]          pc_i,                       // PC of instruction accessing the CSR
43:     output exception_t            csr_exception_o,            // attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege
44:                                                               // level or to write  a read-only register also
45:                                                               // raises illegal instruction exceptions.
46:     // Interrupts/Exceptions
47:     output logic  [63:0]          epc_o,                      // Output the exception PC to PC Gen, the correct CSR (mepc, sepc) is set accordingly
48:     output logic                  eret_o,                     // Return from exception, set the PC of epc_o
49:     output logic  [63:0]          trap_vector_base_o,         // Output base of exception vector, correct CSR is output (mtvec, stvec)
50:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      priv_lvl_o,                 // Current privilege level the CPU is in
51:     // FPU
52:     output riscv::xs_t            fs_o,                       // Floating point extension status
53:     output logic [4:0]            fflags_o,                   // Floating-Point Accured Exceptions
54:     output logic [2:0]            frm_o,                      // Floating-Point Dynamic Rounding Mode
55:     output logic [6:0]            fprec_o,                    // Floating-Point Precision Control
56:     // MMU
57:     output logic                  en_translation_o,           // enable VA translation
58:     output logic                  en_ld_st_translation_o,     // enable VA translation for load and stores
59:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      ld_st_priv_lvl_o,           // Privilege level at which load and stores should happen
60:     output logic                  sum_o,
61:     output logic                  mxr_o,
62:     output logic [43:0]           satp_ppn_o,
63:     output logic [ASID_WIDTH-1:0] asid_o,
64:     // external interrupts
65:     input  logic [1:0]            irq_i,                      // external interrupt in
66:     input  logic                  ipi_i,                      // inter processor interrupt -> connected to machine mode sw
67:     input  logic                  debug_req_i,                // debug request in
68:     output logic                  set_debug_pc_o,
69:     // Virtualization Support
70:     output logic                  tvm_o,                      // trap virtual memory
71:     output logic                  tw_o,                       // timeout wait
72:     output logic                  tsr_o,                      // trap sret
73:     output logic                  debug_mode_o,               // we are in debug mode -> that will change some decoding
74:     output logic                  single_step_o,              // we are in single-step mode
75:     // Caches
76:     output logic                  icache_en_o,                // L1 ICache Enable
77:     output logic                  dcache_en_o,                // L1 DCache Enable
78:     // Performance Counter
79:     output logic  [11:0]          perf_addr_o,                // address to performance counter module
80:     output logic  [63:0]          perf_data_o,                // write data to performance counter module
81:     input  logic  [63:0]          perf_data_i,                // read data from performance counter module
82:     output logic                  perf_we_o,
83:     input  logic                  umode_i
84: );
85:     // internal signal to keep track of access exceptions
86:     logic        read_access_exception, update_access_exception;
87:     logic        csr_we, csr_read;
88:     logic [63:0] csr_wdata, csr_rdata;
89:     riscv::priv_lvl_t   trap_to_priv_lvl;
90:     // register for enabling load store address translation, this is critical, hence the register
91:     logic        en_ld_st_translation_d, en_ld_st_translation_q;
92:     logic  mprv;
93:     logic  mret;  // return from M-mode exception
94:     logic  sret;  // return from S-mode exception
95:     logic  dret;  // return from debug mode
96:     // CSR write causes us to mark the FPU state as dirty
97:     logic  dirty_fp_state_csr;
98:     riscv::csr_t  csr_addr;
99:     // ----------------
100:     // Assignments
101:     // ----------------
102:     assign csr_addr = riscv::csr_t'(csr_addr_i);
103:     assign fs_o = mstatus_q.fs;
104:     // ----------------
105:     // CSR Registers
106:     // ----------------
107:     // privilege level register
108:     riscv::priv_lvl_t   priv_lvl_d, priv_lvl_q;
109:     // we are in debug
110:     logic        debug_mode_q, debug_mode_d;
111: 
112:     riscv::status_rv64_t  mstatus_q,  mstatus_d;
113:     riscv::satp_t         satp_q, satp_d;
114:     riscv::dcsr_t         dcsr_q,     dcsr_d;
115: 
116:     logic        mtvec_rst_load_q;// used to determine whether we came out of reset
117: 
118:     logic [63:0] dpc_q,       dpc_d;
119:     logic [63:0] dscratch0_q, dscratch0_d;
120:     logic [63:0] dscratch1_q, dscratch1_d;
121:     logic [63:0] mtvec_q,     mtvec_d;
122:     logic [63:0] medeleg_q,   medeleg_d;
123:     logic [63:0] mideleg_q,   mideleg_d;
124:     logic [63:0] mip_q,       mip_d;
125:     logic [63:0] mie_q,       mie_d;
126:     logic [63:0] mscratch_q,  mscratch_d;
127:     logic [63:0] mepc_q,      mepc_d;
128:     logic [63:0] mcause_q,    mcause_d;
129:     logic [63:0] mtval_q,     mtval_d;
130: 
131:     logic [63:0] stvec_q,     stvec_d;
132:     logic [63:0] sscratch_q,  sscratch_d;
133:     logic [63:0] sepc_q,      sepc_d;
134:     logic [63:0] scause_q,    scause_d;
135:     logic [63:0] stval_q,     stval_d;
136:     logic [63:0] dcache_q,    dcache_d;
137:     logic [63:0] icache_q,    icache_d;
138: 
139:     logic        wfi_d,       wfi_q;
140: 
141:     logic [63:0] cycle_q,     cycle_d;
142:     logic [63:0] instret_q,   instret_d;
143: 
144:     riscv::fcsr_t fcsr_q, fcsr_d;
145: 
146:     // ----------------
147:     // CSR Read logic
148:     // ----------------
149:     always_comb begin : csr_read_process
150:         // a read access exception can only occur if we attempt to read a CSR which does not exist
151:         read_access_exception = 1'b0;
152:         csr_rdata = 64'b0;
153:         // feed through address of performance counter
154:         perf_addr_o = csr_addr.address;
155: 
156:         if (csr_read) begin
157:             unique case (csr_addr.address)
158:                 riscv::CSR_FFLAGS: begin
159:                     if (mstatus_q.fs == riscv::Off) begin
160:                         read_access_exception = 1'b1;
161:                     end else begin

365:                 end
366:                 riscv::CSR_FTRAN: begin
367:                     if (mstatus_q.fs == riscv::Off) begin
368:                         update_access_exception = 1'b1;
369:                     end else begin
370:                         dirty_fp_state_csr = 1'b1;
371:                         fcsr_d.fprec = csr_wdata[6:0]; // ignore writes to reserved space
372:                         // this instruction has side-effects
373:                         flush_o = 1'b1;
374:                     end
375:                 end
376:                 // debug CSR
377:                 riscv::CSR_DCSR: begin
378:                     dcsr_d = csr_wdata[31:0];
379:                     // debug is implemented
380:                     dcsr_d.xdebugver = 4'h4;
381:                     // privilege level

525:             fcsr_d.fflags = csr_wdata_i[4:0] | fcsr_q.fflags;
526:         end
527:         // ---------------------
528:         // External Interrupts
529:         // ---------------------
530:         // Machine Mode External Interrupt Pending
531:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_EXT] = irq_i[0];
532:         // Machine software interrupt
533:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_SOFT] = ipi_i;
534:         // Timer interrupt pending, coming from platform timer
535:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_TIMER] = time_irq_i;
536: 
537:         // -----------------------
538:         // Manage Exception Stack
539:         // -----------------------
540:         // update exception CSRs
541:         // we got an exception update cause, pc and stval register
542:         trap_to_priv_lvl = riscv::PRIV_LVL_M;
543:         // Exception is taken and we are not in debug mode
544:         // exceptions in debug mode don't update any fields
545:         if (!debug_mode_q && ex_i.valid) begin

849:         // -----------------

853:             //if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl)) begin
854:             if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) && !(csr_addr.address==riscv::CSR_MEPC)) begin   
855:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
856:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
857:             end
858:             // check access to debug mode only CSRs
859:             if (csr_addr_i[11:4] == 8'h7b && !debug_mode_q) begin
860:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
861:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
862:             end
863:         end

466:     .fence_i_o              ( fence_i_commit_controller     ),

468:     .sfence_vma_o           ( sfence_vma_commit_controller  ),
469:     .flush_commit_o         ( flush_commit                  ),
470:     .*
471:   );

75:     // Caches

82:     output logic                  perf_we_o,

29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception

35:

54:     output logic [2:0]            frm_o,                      // Floating-Point Dynamic Rounding Mode
55:     output logic [6:0]            fprec_o,                    // Floating-Point Precision Control
56:     // MMU

58:     output logic                  en_ld_st_translation_o,     // enable VA translation for load and stores
59:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      ld_st_priv_lvl_o,           // Privilege level at which load and stores should happen

66:     input  logic                  ipi_i,                      // inter processor interrupt -> connected to machine mode sw
67:     input  logic                  debug_req_i,                // debug request in

71:     output logic                  tw_o,                       // timeout wait
72:     output logic                  tsr_o,                      // trap sret
73:     output logic                  debug_mode_o,               // we are in debug mode -> that will change some decoding
74:     output logic                  single_step_o,              // we are in single-step mode
75:     // Caches
76:     output logic                  icache_en_o,                // L1 ICache Enable
77:     output logic                  dcache_en_o,                // L1 DCache Enable
78:     // Performance Counter
79:     output logic  [11:0]          perf_addr_o,                // address to performance counter module
80:     output logic  [63:0]          perf_data_o,                // write data to performance counter module
81:     input  logic  [63:0]          perf_data_i,                // read data from performance counter module
82:     output logic                  perf_we_o,
83:     input  logic                  umode_i
84: );

15: import ariane_pkg::*;
16: 
17: module csr_regfile #(
18:     parameter int          ASID_WIDTH      = 1,
19:     parameter int unsigned NR_COMMIT_PORTS = 2
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
22:     input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
24:     // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
25:     output logic                  flush_o,
26:     output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested
27:     // commit acknowledge
28:     input  scoreboard_entry_t [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0] commit_instr_i, // the instruction we want to commit
29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception
34:     input exception_t             ex_i,                       // We've got an exception from the commit stage, take its
35: 
36:     input  fu_op                  csr_op_i,                   // Operation to perform on the CSR file
37:     input  logic  [11:0]          csr_addr_i,                 // Address of the register to read/write
38:     input  logic  [63:0]          csr_wdata_i,                // Write data in
39:     output logic  [63:0]          csr_rdata_o,                // Read data out
40:     input  logic                  dirty_fp_state_i,           // Mark the FP sate as dirty
41:     input  logic                  csr_write_fflags_i,         // Write fflags register e.g.: we are retiring a floating point instruction
42:     input  logic  [63:0]          pc_i,                       // PC of instruction accessing the CSR
43:     output exception_t            csr_exception_o,            // attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege
44:                                                               // level or to write  a read-only register also
45:                                                               // raises illegal instruction exceptions.
46:     // Interrupts/Exceptions
47:     output logic  [63:0]          epc_o,                      // Output the exception PC to PC Gen, the correct CSR (mepc, sepc) is set accordingly
48:     output logic                  eret_o,                     // Return from exception, set the PC of epc_o
49:     output logic  [63:0]          trap_vector_base_o,         // Output base of exception vector, correct CSR is output (mtvec, stvec)
50:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      priv_lvl_o,                 // Current privilege level the CPU is in
51:     // FPU
52:     output riscv::xs_t            fs_o,                       // Floating point extension status
53:     output logic [4:0]            fflags_o,                   // Floating-Point Accured Exceptions
54:     output logic [2:0]            frm_o,                      // Floating-Point Dynamic Rounding Mode
55:     output logic [6:0]            fprec_o,                    // Floating-Point Precision Control
56:     // MMU
57:     output logic                  en_translation_o,           // enable VA translation
58:     output logic                  en_ld_st_translation_o,     // enable VA translation for load and stores
59:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      ld_st_priv_lvl_o,           // Privilege level at which load and stores should happen
60:     output logic                  sum_o,
61:     output logic                  mxr_o,
62:     output logic [43:0]           satp_ppn_o,
63:     output logic [ASID_WIDTH-1:0] asid_o,
64:     // external interrupts
65:     input  logic [1:0]            irq_i,                      // external interrupt in
66:     input  logic                  ipi_i,                      // inter processor interrupt -> connected to machine mode sw
67:     input  logic                  debug_req_i,                // debug request in
68:     output logic                  set_debug_pc_o,
69:     // Virtualization Support
70:     output logic                  tvm_o,                      // trap virtual memory
71:     output logic                  tw_o,                       // timeout wait
72:     output logic                  tsr_o,                      // trap sret
73:     output logic                  debug_mode_o,               // we are in debug mode -> that will change some decoding
74:     output logic                  single_step_o,              // we are in single-step mode
75:     // Caches
76:     output logic                  icache_en_o,                // L1 ICache Enable
77:     output logic                  dcache_en_o,                // L1 DCache Enable
78:     // Performance Counter
79:     output logic  [11:0]          perf_addr_o,                // address to performance counter module
80:     output logic  [63:0]          perf_data_o,                // write data to performance counter module
81:     input  logic  [63:0]          perf_data_i,                // read data from performance counter module
82:     output logic                  perf_we_o,
83:     input  logic                  umode_i
84: );
85:     // internal signal to keep track of access exceptions
86:     logic        read_access_exception, update_access_exception;
87:     logic        csr_we, csr_read;
88:     logic [63:0] csr_wdata, csr_rdata;
89:     riscv::priv_lvl_t   trap_to_priv_lvl;
90:     // register for enabling load store address translation, this is critical, hence the register
91:     logic        en_ld_st_translation_d, en_ld_st_translation_q;
92:     logic  mprv;
93:     logic  mret;  // return from M-mode exception
94:     logic  sret;  // return from S-mode exception
95:     logic  dret;  // return from debug mode
96:     // CSR write causes us to mark the FPU state as dirty
97:     logic  dirty_fp_state_csr;
98:     riscv::csr_t  csr_addr;
99:     // ----------------
100:     // Assignments
101:     // ----------------
102:     assign csr_addr = riscv::csr_t'(csr_addr_i);
103:     assign fs_o = mstatus_q.fs;
104:     // ----------------
105:     // CSR Registers
106:     // ----------------
107:     // privilege level register
108:     riscv::priv_lvl_t   priv_lvl_d, priv_lvl_q;
109:     // we are in debug
110:     logic        debug_mode_q, debug_mode_d;
111: 
112:     riscv::status_rv64_t  mstatus_q,  mstatus_d;
113:     riscv::satp_t         satp_q, satp_d;
114:     riscv::dcsr_t         dcsr_q,     dcsr_d;
115: 
116:     logic        mtvec_rst_load_q;// used to determine whether we came out of reset
117: 
118:     logic [63:0] dpc_q,       dpc_d;
119:     logic [63:0] dscratch0_q, dscratch0_d;
120:     logic [63:0] dscratch1_q, dscratch1_d;
121:     logic [63:0] mtvec_q,     mtvec_d;
122:     logic [63:0] medeleg_q,   medeleg_d;
123:     logic [63:0] mideleg_q,   mideleg_d;
124:     logic [63:0] mip_q,       mip_d;
125:     logic [63:0] mie_q,       mie_d;
126:     logic [63:0] mscratch_q,  mscratch_d;
127:     logic [63:0] mepc_q,      mepc_d;
128:     logic [63:0] mcause_q,    mcause_d;
129:     logic [63:0] mtval_q,     mtval_d;
130: 
131:     logic [63:0] stvec_q,     stvec_d;
132:     logic [63:0] sscratch_q,  sscratch_d;
133:     logic [63:0] sepc_q,      sepc_d;
134:     logic [63:0] scause_q,    scause_d;
135:     logic [63:0] stval_q,     stval_d;
136:     logic [63:0] dcache_q,    dcache_d;
137:     logic [63:0] icache_q,    icache_d;
138: 
139:     logic        wfi_d,       wfi_q;
140: 
141:     logic [63:0] cycle_q,     cycle_d;
142:     logic [63:0] instret_q,   instret_d;
143: 
144:     riscv::fcsr_t fcsr_q, fcsr_d;
145: 
146:     // ----------------
147:     // CSR Read logic
148:     // ----------------
149:     always_comb begin : csr_read_process
150:         // a read access exception can only occur if we attempt to read a CSR which does not exist
151:         read_access_exception = 1'b0;
152:         csr_rdata = 64'b0;
153:         // feed through address of performance counter
154:         perf_addr_o = csr_addr.address;
155: 
156:         if (csr_read) begin
157:             unique case (csr_addr.address)
158:                 riscv::CSR_FFLAGS: begin
159:                     if (mstatus_q.fs == riscv::Off) begin
160:                         read_access_exception = 1'b1;
161:                     end else begin

365:                 end
366:                 riscv::CSR_FTRAN: begin
367:                     if (mstatus_q.fs == riscv::Off) begin
368:                         update_access_exception = 1'b1;
369:                     end else begin
370:                         dirty_fp_state_csr = 1'b1;
371:                         fcsr_d.fprec = csr_wdata[6:0]; // ignore writes to reserved space
372:                         // this instruction has side-effects
373:                         flush_o = 1'b1;
374:                     end
375:                 end
376:                 // debug CSR
377:                 riscv::CSR_DCSR: begin
378:                     dcsr_d = csr_wdata[31:0];
379:                     // debug is implemented
380:                     dcsr_d.xdebugver = 4'h4;
381:                     // privilege level
